from scipy import stats
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def task_func(data_dict, data_keys):
    if len(data_keys) != 2:
        raise ValueError("Two data series are required")

    # Calculate correlation coefficient
    x, y = data_dict[data_keys[0]], data_dict[data_keys[1]]
    correlation_coefficient, _ = stats.pearsonr(x, y)

    # Create scatter plot
    plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
    plt.scatter(x, y)
    plt.xlabel(data_keys[0])
    plt.ylabel(data_keys[1])
    plt.title("Scatter Plot of {} and {} with Correlation Coefficient = {:.2f}".format(data_keys[0], data_keys[1], correlation_coefficient))

    # Return the correlation coefficient and the scatter plot
    return (correlation_coefficient, plt.gca())
